string.text: You are using an older version of MagsRedux. Update to Github version for best experience.
Display the ammo status text after checking. Turn OFF to play only the animation with no on-screen message.
Hide Message Only on Inspect Animation

When ON, the ammo message is hidden ONLY for weapons that play a proper inspect animation (anm_ammo_check). Weapons that fall back to fake reload, bolt cycle, lower weapon, or hide/show still display the message. Requires "Show Ammo Message" to be ON.
Enable color-coded messages based on ammo level (red = low, white = full).

Also color the ammo type name and tag by the ammo's own color (AP, HP, FMJ, etc). With this off, the ammo type/tag stays white everywhere while the level message keeps its color. Needs Colored Messages enabled.
Adjust the brightness of colored messages. Lower values produce dimmer colors.
Choose how ammo count is displayed: text description, numeric count, both, or Tarkov-like (a rough "Approx. N" rounded to the nearest 5, or "Full"/"Empty").

Replace the ammo message with a minimalist 6-segment block bar (filled blocks for remaining ammo) instead of text or numbers. Handles "1 in the chamber" (+), empty/no magazine (X______), jammed (X) and grenade (o) too. Overrides Display Mode. Colored Messages still tints the bar by ammo level.

Draw the ammo message as a styled HUD overlay (selectable font, size and screen position, with a drop shadow) instead of the default top-left pickup line. Works with Colored Messages, Display Mode, Minimalist Bar, ammo type/tag and the second highlight line.
Font family used by Custom Text Render.
Font size used by Custom Text Render.
Where the Custom Text Render message appears on screen.
Distance (pixels) from the crosshair to the message, for the "Near Cursor" render positions. No effect on the other positions.
Display the ammo type name in the message (e.g. "9x19 BP", "5.45x39 PS").
Append ammo classification tag to the message (e.g. (AP), (HP), (FMJ), (SL), (BK)).

On the "1 in the chamber" messages (only a chambered round left, no or empty magazine), also show what that chambered round is. Needs Magazines Redux with "keep round in chamber" (retain round) enabled.

When the chambered round is a different ammo type than the magazine top, show both inline at any fill level as "<level> (mag) + 1 (chamber)", e.g. "Full (9x18 FMJ) + 1 (9x18 AP)" or "About Half (9x18 FMJ) + 1 (9x18 AP)". In Tarkov-like display mode the chamber part shows only its tag, e.g. "About Half (9x18 FMJ) + 1 (AP)". When both match, the normal message is shown. Needs Magazines Redux with "keep round in chamber" (retain round) enabled.

Color the message based on ammo type. None: use ammo level color. Whole message: color entire line by ammo type. Separate lines: show ammo tag on second line with its color. Combined: keep the ammo-level color on the main text/bar AND color the ammo type/tag (inline with Custom Text Render; on a second line otherwise).
How long the ammo status message stays on screen (in seconds).
Hide the default HUD ammo counter for a more immersive experience.
Hide the ammo type icon from the HUD.
Allow ammo checking while the weapon is in grenade launcher mode.
Automatically display ammo status after completing a reload.
Master switch for all animations. When OFF, only the text message will be displayed (no weapon animations).

Animation for weapons WITHOUT proper mag inspect animation. Default: hide/show weapon. Lower Weapon: lower weapon briefly. Fake Reload: play reload animation for magazine weapons, special animations for internal magazine weapons.

Animation used when checking ammo while in grenade-launcher mode. Default is Lower Weapon, because the normal path plays the grenade-load animation, which is confusing. Needs "Check In Grenade Mode" enabled.

Animation when NO magazine is loaded (MagsRedux detachable mag weapons only). Hide/Show: switch weapon. Shake/Lower: a short weapon shake (or a lower on weapons without an inspect animation). None: just show message.
Fallback animation for internal magazine weapons (shotguns, revolvers, etc.) not in special lists when using Fake Reload mode.

Animation to play when checking ammo with an empty magazine inserted. "Reload Animation" plays the full reload animation without actually loading ammo. "Animate" tries anm_bore/anm_idle. "Lower Weapon" just lowers the weapon briefly.
Treat 1 in a Chamber as Non-Empty Mag

By default (OFF), a weapon with only 1 round left (just the chambered round, magazine itself empty) plays anm_ammo_check_empty - the magazine is empty after all. Enable this to revert to the engine default: anm_ammo_check (full inspect) still plays because total ammo > 0. Only affects weapons that define anm_ammo_check_empty in their HUD config.
For INTERNAL-mag bolt-action rifles (Kar98, Mosin, etc.) play bolt open/close animation instead of fallback. Does not affect external-mag variants (see option below).
Force Bolt Cycle for External-Mag Rifles

By default, bolt-action rifles with detachable magazines (L96A1, Remington 700, K98 Mauser Kit, etc.) play fake reload (anm_reload). Enable this to force them to play the explicit bolt cycle animation (anm_open/anm_close) instead. Useful if you prefer consistent bolt-cycling for all bolt-action rifles regardless of magazine type.
Just like in real life. When cycling the bolt, eject one round from the magazine (adds to inventory). Requires "Cycle Bolt" to be enabled. Not all bolt-action rifles supported.
For pump-action shotguns (Remington 870, etc.) play pump open/close animation instead of fallback.
Just like in real life. When racking the slide, eject one shell from the magazine (adds to inventory). Requires "Rack Slide" to be enabled. Not all pump-action shotguns supported.

Use blacklist to skip fake reload animations for weapons with non-immersive or broken animations. Affected weapons will use the fallback animation instead. Edit ammo_check_weapons.script to customize the list.
Delay before showing the message (in seconds).
Prevent the reload key from interrupting the ammo check animation while it plays. Turn OFF to allow cancelling the check by reloading.
Ammo Check key cannot be the Reload key - cleared. Use One-Key Weapon Controls for reload-triggered checks.

Key to press for checking ammo. Can be combined with modifier keys. It cannot be the Reload key: that would fire a check on every reload and the animations would fight. To check ammo from the reload key, enable One-Key Weapon Controls instead.
Update MCM to change keybind here. Key defaults to T.
Enable debug mode for animation testing. When ON: Numpad 0-9 tests weapon states, Numpad * lists weapon animations. Use to discover which state triggers bolt cycling.
Hold a modifier and press Reload for a faster reload that DROPS the old magazine on the ground instead of keeping it (a tactical speed reload).

Requires the Magazines mod (Anomaly Magazines Redux) AND Dynamic Reload Speeds. Dynamic Reload Speeds drives the sped-up animation and keeps the reload sound in sync; without it Fast Reload does nothing.
Master switch for the fast reload shortcut. OFF by default. Stands down while One-Key Weapon Controls is enabled (it owns the Reload key).
Hold this modifier together with the Reload key to do a fast reload. A plain Reload (no modifier) stays normal.
Animation speed multiplier for the fast reload (1.25 = 25% faster). Kept modest because the reload sound is not sped up with it.

When loading a Mags Madness "fast mag" (it already speeds the reload by its own amount, up to about 35%), the fast reload boost above is throttled so the COMBINED reload speed does not exceed this multiplier (1.5 = at most 50% faster total). Our boost is never reduced below the fast mag's own speed; if this value is lower than the fast mag's multiplier, the fast reload simply adds nothing. No effect on normal mags.

Block the fast reload when you have no other magazine equipped, so you never throw away your only one. With this off, a fast reload with no spare will still drop the loaded mag. A normal reload is unaffected.
Which way the dropped magazine is tossed, relative to where you are facing. "Drop" disables the toss (mag just falls at your feet).
How hard the magazine is thrown (0 = plain drop, 100 = strong toss). Has no effect when Throw Direction is set to Drop.
Opposite of Fast Reload: makes every normal (non-fast) reload animation slower. Fast reloads are unaffected. Off by default.

How much slower a normal reload plays, in percent (0 = no change, 25 = 25% slower). Needs "Slow Down Normal Reloads" enabled. Recommended maximum is 10-15%: going further desyncs the animation from the reload sound, which keeps playing at its original speed.

Multiplex a single key into several weapon actions: tap to reload, tap + modifier to unjam, double-tap to switch ammo type, hold to check ammo. Magazine actions use Anomaly Magazines Redux when installed, otherwise they fall back to the engine reload.
Master switch for this feature. OFF by default so the Reload key behaves normally. Turn ON to route reload, unjam, ammo switch and ammo check through one key.

The key that drives all One Key actions. Must NOT be the same key as your vanilla Reload control, or the engine will also reload on every press. Recommended setup: rebind vanilla Reload to a free key, then set this to your old Reload key (R).
When ON, a plain tap on a jammed weapon unjams it instead of reloading (no modifier needed). When the weapon is not jammed the tap reloads as usual. Requires Weapon Parts Overhaul (arti_jamming).
When ON, pressing the Action Key while the PDA is in hand raises or lowers the PDA, so the same key toggles the device.

What a single tap does. Holding the modifier and tapping always unjams (and takes priority over this). Situational: ammo wheel when 3+ types are available, swap to next when exactly 2, reload when fewer. Next Ammo Type: cycle to the next available type. Ammo Wheel: always open the wheel. Reload: just reload. Fast Reload: speed reload that drops the old mag. Ammo Check: play the inspect/check.

What a double-tap does. Situational: ammo wheel when 3+ types are available, swap to next when exactly 2, reload when fewer. Next Ammo Type: cycle to the next available type. Ammo Wheel: always open the wheel. Reload: just reload. Ammo Check: play the inspect/check.

What holding the key does. Ammo Check: play the inspect/check. Reload: just reload. Fast Reload: speed reload that drops the old mag. Next Ammo Type: cycle to the next type. Ammo Wheel: open the wheel. Situational: wheel/next/reload by how many types are available. None: holding does nothing, the press still runs the tap action on release.
How long to hold the key before it triggers the ammo check instead of a reload. Lower feels snappier; too low may fire a check on a slightly long tap. Default 200.

Time window for a second tap to count as a double-tap. Also how long a single tap-reload waits before firing, so a quick second tap can convert it to the ammo-type switch. Lower makes reload snappier but shrinks the double-tap window. Default 200.
Update MCM to 1.6.0 or newer to rebind the action key here. Key defaults to R.
One key to cycle the weapon fire mode and show the new mode on screen. Modifier + key cycles backwards. Hold the key to show the current mode. Uses its own key (default X), separate from reload.
Master switch for the fire mode key. OFF by default.
Key that cycles the fire mode. Tap to go to the next mode, modifier + tap for the previous, hold to show the current mode.
Hide the default on-screen fire mode indicator, so only this mod's message shows the mode. Needs the One-Key Fire Mode feature for the message.

Tint the on-screen fire mode text by mode: green for single, amber for burst, red for full-auto. Uses the custom text rendering when that is enabled. OFF shows plain white text. Tints the Text and None styles, and the appended letter in the Dots styles; the dots themselves stay white. In the Ready or Not styles it switches the selector graphic between red (ON) and white (OFF).

How to show the fire mode. Text: word labels. None: nothing (pair with Display Fire Mode Letter to show just the letter). Dots: four dots, white for active and dark for empty (single 1, burst N, full-auto 4). Dots alt.: only the white active dots, no padding. Custom Texture: a fire-selector graphic chosen in the Fire Mode Texture option below. The two-tone dark dots need Custom Text Rendering; without it empty dots show as 'o'.

Which selector graphic the Custom Texture display style draws. Ready or Not: a fire-selector with baked mode text. Ready or Not (no text): the same graphic without the text. Color ON draws it red, OFF white. BWais's texture: uses the atlas from BWais FireModeCheck (that mod must be installed) and is tinted by the Hue/Opacity options. The selector body (with or without a burst position) is derived from the weapon's own fire modes, so it works for any weapon. Only used when Display Style is set to Custom Texture.

Where the fire mode shows. Default: follow the AmmoCheck message position (Display settings). Screen Bottom: pin to the very bottom edge, below the bottom-center. Applies to all styles including Ready or Not. The text and dots styles only move when Custom Text Rendering is on; the plain message line always sits at the bottom.
Append the mode letter to the message: [S] single, [B] burst, [A] auto. Works in the text, none and dots styles. With the None style it is the only thing shown.

Hold the fire-mode key to play a weapon inspection animation instead of showing the current fire mode. Tapping the key still cycles the fire mode. Falls back to showing the mode if the weapon has no inspect animation.
Transparency of the selected fire mode texture. 255 is fully opaque, 0 is invisible. Only applies to the Custom Texture display style.

Color tint for the selected fire mode texture. 0 leaves the texture's own color. 1-360 applies a hue. Use the no-text (white) variant for a clean tint; the red texture fights a colored tint. Only applies to the Custom Texture display style.

Nudge the custom texture up or down. Negative moves it up, positive moves it down (pixels in the 1024x768 UI space). Use it to fine-tune the position, for example to push the graphic right to the screen bottom. Only applies to the Custom Texture display style.
Hold this modifier and tap the One Key to unjam the weapon instead of reloading. Needs Weapon Parts Overhaul (arti_jamming).
Hold this modifier and tap the Fire Mode key to cycle to the PREVIOUS fire mode instead of the next.
Optional modifier to hold together with the Ammo Check key. None means the key works on its own.
How the Ammo Check key activates: Press (single tap), Double-Tap, or Hold.
